title: What is MATLAB?
body: MATLAB is a tool for math, data, and engineering. You type a command and it computes the result instantly. The '%' symbol starts a comment, which MATLAB ignores.
code:
% This is a comment
5 + 3        % MATLAB shows: ans = 8

title: Variables & Assignment
body: A variable stores a value under a name so you can reuse it. The '=' sign means 'store the right side into the name on the left'. Ending a line with ';' hides the output.
code:
x = 5;        % store 5 in x
y = x * 2;    % y is now 10
y             % shows: y = 10

title: Vectors & Arrays
body: MATLAB is built around lists of numbers called vectors (or arrays). You make one with square brackets. Indexing starts at 1, not 0.

title: Doing Math
body: MATLAB does normal math with + - * /. To do math on each element of a vector, put a dot before the operator (.* ./ .^).

title: for Loops
body: A 'for' loop repeats code a set number of times. '1:5' means the numbers 1 through 5. 'disp' displays a value. Every for needs a matching 'end'.
code:
for i = 1:5
    disp(i)   % shows 1, then 2, ... up to 5
end

title: Milestone: Plotting a Graph
body: Putting it together: make an x vector, compute a y vector from it, and draw it. 'plot(x, y)' opens a graph. You just turned numbers into a picture.

title: The Shape of a C Program
body: Every C program starts running at 'main'. '#include <stdio.h>' loads tools for input/output. Code goes inside { } and each statement ends with a semicolon.

title: Variables & Types
body: In C you must say what kind of value a variable holds: 'int' for whole numbers, 'double' for decimals, 'char' for a single letter. Declare the type, then the name.

title: Printing with printf
body: 'printf' prints text to the screen. '\n' makes a new line. Use '%d' as a placeholder for an int and pass the value after the text.

title: Making Decisions: if / else
body: 'if' runs code only when a condition is true; 'else' runs when it is false. '==' tests equality (two equals signs), while a single '=' assigns.

title: for Loops
body: A 'for' loop has three parts: start, condition, and update. This one counts i from 0 while i is less than 5, adding 1 each time (i++).

title: Milestone: A Tiny Full Program
body: Everything together: include, main, a variable, a loop, and printf. This prints the numbers 1 to 3 with a label. That is a complete, runnable C program.

title: C++ vs C
body: C++ builds on C: most C code still works. The big additions are easier input/output (cout/cin) and objects/classes. C++ uses '#include <iostream>' for printing.

title: cout and cin
body: 'cout <<' prints values; chain pieces with '<<'. 'endl' ends the line. 'cin >>' reads a value the user types into a variable.

title: Variables & Types
body: Like C, C++ uses types: 'int', 'double', 'char', plus 'string' (with #include <string>) for text and 'bool' for true/false.

title: Functions
body: A function is reusable code with a name. You state the return type, the name, and any inputs (parameters). 'return' sends a value back.

title: if / else and Loops
body: Decisions and repetition work just like C. Here a loop runs while a condition holds, and an if checks a value each time.

title: Milestone: Your First Struct
body: A struct bundles related data into one type. Here a 'Player' has a name and level. You make one and read its fields with a dot.
